plant.description:
Brief Description and Common Forms
Description: Scallions are Allium plants that are strongly aromatic, releasing a distinctive, characteristic odor when cut, crushed, or chewed2. This aroma comes from complex sulfur-containing organic compounds (organosulfoxides) that are released when the plant experiences mechanical trauma24. Unlike standard onions, they are characterized by their long green stalks and lack a massive, fully developed underground bulb3.
Common Forms: Scallions are incredibly versatile and are heavily featured in everyday human dishes, soups, and sauces worldwide23. They are most commonly prepared and consumed fresh (raw), cooked, or dehydrated35.

toxins.name: Because scallions (green onions) belong to the Allium genus
Brief description of its mechanism of action
toxins.description:
It acts as a highly reactive oxidant that causes a marked decrease in the activity of the enzyme glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD) in feline red blood cells57. This decreases the concentration of glutathione, which is necessary to prevent oxidative damage58. As a result, hydrogen peroxide oxidizes the hemoglobin, causing it to denature, precipitate, and form clumps known as Heinz bodies on the cell membrane7.... This leads to the rupture and destruction of the red blood cells (oxidative hemolysis)910.
Any notes on concentration or potency: It is described as a "very poisonous" organosulfur compound and is considered the principal toxin in Allium species5.... Cats are exceptionally vulnerable to this toxin because feline hemoglobin contains eight reactive sulfhydryl groups, making their red blood cells two to three times more susceptible to oxidative damage than those of other species
toxins.concentration_notes: or potency: It is described as a "very poisonous" organosulfur compound and is considered the principal toxin in Allium species5.... Cats are exceptionally vulnerable to this toxin because feline hemoglobin contains eight reactive sulfhydryl groups, making their red blood cells two to three times more susceptible to oxidative damage than those of other species

toxins.name: Sodium n-propylthiosulfate
toxins.description:
Similar to N-propyl disulfide, it reduces glutathione concentrations in erythrocytes and significantly increases methemoglobin concentrations and Heinz body counts57. This oxidative damage ultimately leads to cell lysis and hemolytic anemia712.
Any notes on concentration or potency: This is also a "very poisonous" compound57. Notably, it has been isolated specifically from boiled Allium materials, demonstrating that these toxins survive high temperatures and cooking processes
toxins.concentration_notes: or potency: This is also a "very poisonous" compound57. Notably, it has been isolated specifically from boiled Allium materials, demonstrating that these toxins survive high temperatures and cooking processes

toxins.name: Brief description of its mechanism of action
toxins.description:
It is readily absorbed through the cat's gastrointestinal tract and metabolized into highly reactive oxidants that trigger oxidative hemolysis34.
Any notes on concentration or potency: It is another primary causative agent implicated in Allium-induced hemolytic anemia
toxins.concentration_notes: or potency: It is another primary causative agent implicated in Allium-induced hemolytic anemia

toxins.name: Alk(en)yl cysteine sulfoxides (Precursors)
toxins.description:
These are the non-toxic parent compounds sequestered inside the plant's cells213. When the plant experiences mechanical trauma—such as a cat chewing the scallion or a human chopping it—these precursors instantly convert into the highly reactive, cell-damaging sulfur-containing organic compounds listed above2....
Any notes on concentration or potency: These compounds are responsible for the characteristic flavor and odor of scallions413. Because they are present throughout the plant tissue and convert to toxins instantly upon damage, no part of the scallion is safe for a cat
toxins.concentration_notes: or potency: These compounds are responsible for the characteristic flavor and odor of scallions413. Because they are present throughout the plant tissue and convert to toxins instantly upon damage, no part of the scallion is safe for a cat

symptoms.name: Because scallions (green onions) are members of the Allium genus
Vomiting, Diarrhea, Abdominal Pain, and Drooling
symptoms.notes: These are usually the first clinical signs of toxicosis to appear4.... They are caused by direct irritation to the mouth and stomach, frequently resulting in dehydration

symptoms.name: Lethargy, Weakness, and Depression
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days (typically 2 to 4 days).
symptoms.notes: These signs emerge as a secondary response to systemic hypoxia caused by the profound loss of oxygen-carrying red blood cells

symptoms.name: Pale Mucous Membranes and Jaundice (Icterus)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days.
symptoms.notes: Pale gums indicate a lack of oxygenated blood (anemia)5.... Jaundice (yellowing of the skin or mucosa) occurs due to severe red blood cell destruction leading to hyperbilirubinemia

symptoms.name: Tachypnea and Dyspnea (Breathing Difficulties)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days.
symptoms.notes: This is a compensatory response as the cat's body attempts to offset the decreased blood oxygen transportation capacity and resulting tissue hypoxia

symptoms.name: Tachycardia (Rapid Heart Rate)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days.
symptoms.notes: Tachycardia develops as the heart pumps much faster to compensate for the impaired oxygen transportation caused by the ruptured red blood cells

symptoms.name: Hemoglobinuria (Dark Urine)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days.
symptoms.notes: This is a hallmark sign of severe oxidative hemolysis5.... Damaged red blood cells rupture and release hemoglobin, which is then excreted through the urinary tract59. This can lead to secondary acute kidney injury (hemoglobinuric nephrosis)

symptoms.name: Cold Sensitivity
symptoms.notes: Affected cats may have difficulty regulating body temperature as a systemic effect of the progressive anemia

symptoms.name: Allium Breath Odor
symptoms.notes: While not harmful itself, this symptom is specifically associated with recent ingestion and serves as a highly valuable diagnostic clue for veterinarians

treatments.name: Because scallions and green onions belong to the Allium genus, they cause the exact same oxidative damage and toxicosis as onions and garlic. Since there is no specific antidote for Allium poisoning
Oxygen Therapy and Blood Transfusions (Life-Saving Stabilization)
treatments.description:
of the procedure: Providing the cat with supplemental oxygen and transfusing whole blood to restore the body's depleted oxygen-carrying capacity1....
Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: This is the most urgent priority for severely affected cats presenting with life-threatening signs such as respiratory distress, systemic hypoxia, or profound hemolytic anemia (specifically when the hematocrit drops below 15–20%)

treatments.name: Induction of Emesis (Early Gastrointestinal Decontamination)
treatments.description:
of the procedure: Administering an emetic drug, such as dexmedetomidine (an α
2
	​

-adrenergic agonist), via intramuscular or intravenous injection to force the cat to expel the ingested plant material before the toxins are absorbed78.
Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: This is highly valuable but only indicated if the ingestion was very recent (typically caught within 1 to 3 hours) and the cat is currently asymptomatic1.... Crucially, 3% hydrogen peroxide must never be used to induce vomiting in cats, as it is strictly contraindicated and can cause fatal hemorrhagic gastritis

treatments.name: Administration of Activated Charcoal (Secondary Decontamination)
treatments.description:
of the procedure: Administering oral activated charcoal (typically 1 to 4 g/kg) to bind to and adsorb any residual toxins remaining in the stomach and intestines1....
Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: This is typically administered directly after emesis to trap toxins before they can enter the bloodstream1.... However, it should be noted that its actual efficacy in binding to or preventing the production of Allium-derived sulfides is currently unknown

treatments.name: Intravenous (IV) Fluid Therapy (Systemic Supportive Care)
treatments.description:
of the procedure: Hospitalizing the cat to administer continuous IV fluids to rehydrate the animal, support blood pressure, and maintain proper renal perfusion1....
Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: This treatment is indicated if the cat has suffered extensive fluid loss from severe vomiting and diarrhea, or presents with hypotension1.... Furthermore, it is an essential protective measure if the cat develops hemoglobinuria (dark urine caused by ruptured red blood cells clearing through the urinary tract), as IV diuresis helps protect the kidneys against secondary damage known as hemoglobinuric nephrosis

treatments.name: Gastrointestinal and Antioxidant Medications (Symptomatic Relief)
treatments.description:
of the procedure: Administering anti-sickness/anti-nausea medications, stomach protectants, and sometimes antioxidant vitamins (C and E) or N-acetyl-cysteine1....
Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: GI protectants and anti-sickness medications are used to manage mild-to-moderate abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea9. While antioxidants are sometimes administered in an attempt to combat the oxidative damage to red blood cells, they have shown minimal proven protective effects against Allium powder toxicosis in cats
